name: Graphene (Python GraphQL Framework)

auth.notes: Schema definition library; auth handled by web framework (Django, Flask, FastAPI)

performance.notes: Schema execution overhead is minimal; DB/resolver performance dominates

agent_readiness.notes: GraphQL schema definition and execution for Python. import graphene. class Query(graphene.ObjectType): hello = graphene.String(name=graphene.String(default_value='World')). def resolve_hello(root, info, name): return f'Hello {name}!'. schema = graphene.Schema(query=Query). result = schema.execute('{ hello }'. result.data → {'hello': 'Hello World!'}. Types: ObjectType, Interface, Union, Enum, Scalar, InputObjectType. Mutations: class MyMutation(graphene.Mutation). Integrations: graphene-django (ORM introspection), graphene-sqlalchemy, graphene-pydantic. Relay support. Connection/Edge for pagination. graphene.List, graphene.NonNull. Async resolvers with async def. DataLoader integration for N+1 prevention.

gotchas: Graphene 3 has breaking changes from Graphene 2 — migration required
No type annotations on Graphene classes — relies on Meta class pattern
N+1 query problem is common — requires DataLoader or select_related
Strawberry GraphQL is the modern successor with type annotations
Limited subscription support — use ariadne or strawberry for real-time
best_when: Building GraphQL APIs with Django (graphene-django) or SQLAlchemy
Code-first GraphQL schemas with Python classes
When Relay pagination spec is required
avoid_when: New projects — prefer Strawberry GraphQL (modern, type-annotated)
summary: Graphene is a code-first GraphQL framework for Python. class Query(graphene.ObjectType) with resolver methods. schema = graphene.Schema(query=Query). Integrations for Django ORM and SQLAlchemy. Relay pagination. Async resolvers. MIT by Syrus Akbary. Best for Django GraphQL APIs. Graphene 3 has breaking changes from 2. N+1 queries common — use DataLoader. Strawberry is the modern typed alternative for new projects.
